messages.author: djembejim
messages.text: Anyone looking to collaborate on expanding repeater links across the Wiltshire Berkshire boarder? We’ve established a solid Meshcore Mesh, south of Marlborough to East Grafton, Devizes, Salisbury and Salisbury south. Looking west and north currently but happy to deploy test repeaters if the terrain and coverage looks promising…

messages.author: de-animator
messages.text: Im currently researching what the most effective/powerful equipment is for setting up a repeater on top of my house so that coverage is extended. Not sure if this is enough height but from the ground to the surface on my roof upon which I can stand - is 12m. So adding a pole antenna and pushing for 14-15m should be doable. There aren't any other buildings that tall for quite a distance (not sure how to measure) so I hope this will be enough/useful Does anyone have any recommendations (up to £1k) for equipment to buy?

messages.author: dutchoven
messages.text: Greetings from the west, with the deployment of Hackpen at the weekend, we have linked up NW Wiltshire with Marlborough and Swindon. Looking to do something similar to expand east into Hungerford/Newbury. Anyone interested? @rally_mike (m3sh.uk) @djembejim
messages.content_html: Greetings from the west, with the deployment of Hackpen at the weekend, we have linked up NW Wiltshire with Marlborough and Swindon. Looking to do something similar to expand east into Hungerford/Newbury. Anyone interested? @rally_mike (m3sh.uk) @djembejim

messages.author: djembejim
messages.text: Great job over the wet weekend mate. Repeaters are providing solid links between Swindon and Salisbury. East Grafton owner of SN8 @DA M7CAY has been looking to expand east. Maybe one of the peaks north of Combe, Ink Pen Hill, Summer Hill, Walbury Hill???? (edited)

messages.author: djembejim
messages.text: What’s your location? Not an expert so others can chime in but turning your advert to 24hrs will reduce flood traffic. I think more repeaters in a location help with failover. But we’ll find out how that impacts the Mesh as it scales..

messages.author: S7uart G5AIL
messages.text: Just a heads-up – I’ve been tinkering with something called MeshRank It’s basically a way of giving a bit more visibility into what the mesh is actually doing, without changing how MeshCORE works. No internet routing, no messing with RF, nothing in the message path. It just listens, observes, and feeds back what already happened. Why I built it: • New repeaters often get switched off because it’s hard to tell if they’re helping • People end up spamming test messages just to see if anything’s alive • When stuff doesn’t propagate, there’s no clue why MeshRank gives things like delivery confidence, shows which repeaters are contributing, and highlights where messages are being seen but not going any further. Observers are totally passive — they don’t transmit or interfere in any way. If MeshRank fell over tomorrow, the mesh would carry on exactly the same. I’m looking for a few folks (UK first) who wouldn’t mind running an observer and helping build some early coverage — high ground / urban spots especially useful. If you fancy a look: https://meshrank.net Happy to answer questions or take criticism
messages.content_html: Just a heads-up – I’ve been tinkering with something called MeshRank It’s basically a way of giving a bit more visibility into what the mesh is actually doing, without changing how MeshCORE works. No internet routing, no messing with RF, nothing in the message path. It just listens, observes, and feeds back what already happened. Why I built it: • New repeaters often get switched off because it’s hard to tell if they’re helping • People end up spamming test messages just to see if anything’s alive • When stuff doesn’t propagate, there’s no clue why MeshRank gives things like delivery confidence, shows which repeaters are contributing, and highlights where messages are being seen but not going any further. Observers are totally passive — they don’t transmit or interfere in any way. If MeshRank fell over tomorrow, the mesh would carry on exactly the same. I’m looking for a few folks (UK first) who wouldn’t mind running an observer and helping build some early coverage — high ground / urban spots especially useful. If you fancy a look: https://meshrank.net Happy to answer questions or take criticism
